SEARCH
Module it.polimi.ingsw.am37
Package it.polimi.ingsw.am37.view.clientmodel

Class ClientSideGameModel

java.lang.Object
it.polimi.ingsw.am37.view.clientmodel.ClientSideGameModel
public class ClientSideGameModel extends Object
This class is the client-side representation of the game model. It contains all the information that the client needs to know about the game. It is updated by the server and the client-side controller.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    ClientSideGameModel()
    Creates a new client-side game model.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

  • Constructor Details

    • ClientSideGameModel

      public ClientSideGameModel()
      Creates a new client-side game model. It initializes all the lists of cards and players. It creates a new card creator to create all the cards. It initializes the list of lobbies. It initializes the list of players. It initializes the list of tokens. It initializes the list of resource cards. It initializes the list of gold cards. It initializes the list of objective cards. It initializes the list of start cards. It initializes the list of available resource cards. It initializes the list of available gold cards. It initializes the list of public objectives.

  • Method Details

    • setListener

      public void setListener(PropertyChangeListener listener)
      Sets the listener of the model.
      Parameters:
      listener - the listener to set.

      Returns:

      public List<Integer> getListOfLobbies()
      Gets the list of all the lobbies.
      Returns:
      the list of all the lobbies.

    • setListOfLobbies

      public void setListOfLobbies(List<Integer> listOfLobbies)
      Sets the list of all the lobbies. It notifies the listener that the list of lobbies has changed.
      Parameters:
      listOfLobbies - the list of all the lobbies to set.

    • getNumOfLobby

      public int getNumOfLobby()
      Gets the number of the lobby the player is in.
      Returns:
      the number of the lobby the player is in.

    • setNumOfLobby

      public void setNumOfLobby(int numOfLobby)
      Sets the number of the lobby the player is in.
      Parameters:
      numOfLobby - the number of the lobby to set.

    • getNumOfPlayers

      public int getNumOfPlayers()
      Gets the number of players in the lobby.
      Returns:
      the number of players in the lobby.

    • setNumOfPlayers

      public void setNumOfPlayers(int numOfPlayers)
      Sets the number of players in the lobby.
      Parameters:
      numOfPlayers - the number of players to set.

    • getPlayers

      public List<ClientSidePlayer> getPlayers()
      Gets the list of all the players in the game.
      Returns:
      the list of all the players in the game.

    • addPlayer

      public void addPlayer(ClientSidePlayer newPlayer)
      Adds a player to the list of players. It notifies the listener that a new player has been added.
      Parameters:
      newPlayer - the player to add.

      Gets the player that is currently playing.
      Returns:
      the player that is currently playing.

      Sets the list of all the private objectives in the game.

      privateObjectives - the list of all the private objectives to set.

      public ObjectiveCard getMyPrivateObjective()
      Gets the private objective card that the player has chosen.
      Returns:
      the private objective card that the player has chosen.

      public void setMyPrivateObjective(ObjectiveCard myPrivateObjective)
      Sets the private objective card that the player has chosen.
      Parameters:
      myPrivateObjective - the private objective card that the player has chosen.

      public StartCard getMyStartCard()
      Gets the start card that the player has chosen.

      Sets the start card that the player has chosen.
      Parameters:
      myStartCard - the start card that the player has chosen.

    • getMyHand

      public List<StandardCard> getMyHand()
      Gets the hand of the player.
      Returns:
      the hand of the player.

    • setMyHand

      public void setMyHand(List<StandardCard> myHand)
      Sets the hand of the player.
      Parameters:
      myHand - the hand of the player.

    • initializeKingdom

      public void initializeKingdom(ClientSidePlayer player, StartCard placed, Side placedSide)
      Initializes the kingdom of the player. It notifies the listener that the kingdom of the player has changed.
      Parameters:
      player - the player that is initializing the kingdom.
      placed - the start card that the player has placed.
      placedSide - the side of the start card that the player has placed.

    • placeCard

      public void placeCard(String player, int cardId, String side, Position pos)
      Places a card on the player's kingdom. It notifies the listener that the kingdom has changed. It removes the card from the player's hand. It adds the points given by the card to the player. It updates the player's kingdom. It updates the linked sides of the corners of the placed side. It updates the player's points. It updates the player's kingdom.
      Parameters:
      player - the player that is placing the card.
      cardId - the id of the card to place.
      side - the side of the card to place.
      pos - the position where to place the card.

    • getPlayersInOrder

      public List<String> getPlayersInOrder()
      Gets the list of players in order of turn.
      Returns:
      the list of players in order of turn.

    • setPlayersInOrder

      public void setPlayersInOrder(List<String> playersInOrder)
      Sets the list of players in order of turn. It sets the current player to the first player in the list. It notifies the listener that a new turn has started.
      Parameters:
      playersInOrder - the list of players in order of turn.

    • getCurrentPlayer

      public String getCurrentPlayer()
      Gets the nickname of the player that is currently playing.
      Returns:
      the nickname of the player that is currently playing.

    • nextTurn

      public void nextTurn()
      Changes the turn to the next player. It notifies the listener that a new turn has started. It sets the current player to the next player in the list. If the current player is the last player in the list, it sets the current player to the first player in the list.
